fullArticle:
Bigger portions, fresh bread and tea at checkout help Japan convenience stores perk up

Products such as fresh bread and tea have helped earnings recover for Seven & i's Japanese convenience stores. (Photo by Yuki Nakao)

TOKYO -- Net profit at Japan's Seven & i Holdings, the parent of 7-Eleven, rose 24% on the year to 60.6 billion yen ($373 million) for the March-May quarter, as gasoline sales boosted convenience store profits overseas while domestic sales picked up....
